tax: Arc Stormling is placed at species rank beneath Stormling (Fulgurant order, Air Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a charged cloud-body, branching lightning strikes, and arc-scorch as its trail. It is separated from its sibling the Static Sparkmote by its branching lightning strikes versus the Sparkmote's clinging crawling static; the two share the genus body-plan but never the crawling surface static. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: charged storm-air veined with living electrical discharge.
ap: Arc Stormling stands 2–3 m churning cloud. Its silhouette is a dark charged cloud-body crawling with blue-white arcs. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: crawling blue-white arcs across its surface; a sharp ozone reek and hair-raising charge before it strikes; and a building crackle that peaks just before discharge. It moves with a drifting float along charged air, fastest toward grounded metal, and shows a flickering blue-white arc-light. Even after dispersal it can be identified from scorched arc-marks, fused stone, and an ozone tang along its path, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Arc Stormling persists in charged storm-shafts, ozone-tinged galleries, and metal-veined passages where charge can build and ground. It does not breed; it forms where air-pressure animates a charged storm-air pocket into a coherent body, and recurs whenever the shaft re-charges and the cloud re-coheres. It sustains its mass on atmospheric electrical charge, drawing in charged air and grounding paths drawn into its body. Range is bounded by the charged-air zone: outside that band the body discharges to ground and disperses where charge fails. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a shaft where hair rises and faint arcs crawl with no storm to explain it, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Arc Stormling is roused by grounded metal, a wet crew, or close approach and otherwise lies a still dark cloud posing as charged mist. It claims a single charged shaft and answers intrusion with striking with branching lightning at the best-grounded target. Toward grey-folk it is drawn to metal and wet crews; survey crews report that Wall-Wardens strip and stow metal in any gallery that smells of ozone. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Arc Stormling: a body of charged storm-air veined with living electrical discharge; immune to electricity and to physical attack; and a defining weakness — grounding and damp enclosure, which bleed off its charge. Its senses run through the medium itself: electrical-field sensing; it strikes the best ground. It tolerates darkness, heat, and thin air but fails at grounding-out and damp enclosure. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the charged cloud and arc-veined surface, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Arc Stormling are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it strikes with branching lightning at grounded targets; it builds charge to a stunning discharge; it draws charged air to rebuild its body; and it crawls arcs along metal to reach distant prey. Each is a direct expression of living electrical charge in storm-air, so it cannot, for instance, produce mass-force, persist when grounded out, or cross dead damp air. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Arc Stormling matters because it makes ozone-smelling galleries a lightning hazard, and crews now stow metal before entering. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as an electrical-strike hazard logged with charged shafts, and its presence reshapes routes through the upper charged shafts, now entered metal-stowed and on insulated footing. Rising hair and crawling arcs without a storm are the Wall-Wardens' sign a Stormling has formed.
g: Arc Stormling (Fulgur vivum) is a storm-air elemental that crackles with living charge and strikes with branching lightning. Documented from the charged storm-shafts and ozone-tinged galleries of the upper tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a churning charged cloud-body veined with crawling lightning — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It is air given electrical charge, hunting by the arc rather than the gust. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a fading crackle, a smell of ozone, and scorched arc-marks on the stone and no carcass.
